nodes.title: Publish the bootstrap repository to GitHub
nodes.spec: Create or verify cat-cave/madobe on GitHub, initialize the local repository, push main, and confirm both orchestrators can clone and sync qd state.
nodes.acceptance:
cat-cave/madobe exists on GitHub
main contains the M0 scaffold and runbooks
this machine can pull and push over HTTPS with gh authentication
roadmap/qd-export.json is committed

nodes.title: Create the GitHub orchestrator coordination issue
nodes.spec: Create and pin the GitHub coordination issue used by the Linux and macOS Codex orchestrators for durable cross-machine status, validation requests, blockers, and decisions.
nodes.acceptance:
GitHub issue named Orchestrator Coordination Log exists
Issue body links the general, Linux, macOS, and cross-device runbooks
Initial Linux orchestrator status comment is posted
Coordination labels exist or missing-label follow-up is recorded

nodes.title: Verify the bootstrap scaffold on macOS
nodes.spec: Clone the GitHub repo on the MacBook, sync qd from the committed export, acknowledge the qd method for the macOS orchestrator, and validate Tuist/Xcode and Mac-local checks.
nodes.acceptance:
MacBook can clone cat-cave/madobe over HTTPS
macOS qd sync from roadmap/qd-export.json succeeds
macOS qd method is acknowledged as trevor-mac-orchestrator
nix develop -c just check succeeds or blocker is recorded
nix develop -c just apple-generate and just apple-test succeed or exact blocker is recorded
evidence/m0-mac-bootstrap-verify contains command log and mac-env.txt

nodes.title: Make GitHub CI pass from a clean checkout
nodes.spec: Run the hosted GitHub workflows from the pushed repository, fix CI-only drift, and establish the first green baseline for Linux and macOS jobs.
nodes.acceptance:
GitHub Actions ci workflow runs on main or a bootstrap PR
Linux job passes from clean checkout
macOS job passes from clean checkout or a precise macOS CI blocker is filed
Nightly workflow is syntactically valid
CI result URL is recorded in evidence/m0-ci-green/notes.md

nodes.title: Prove the two-orchestrator qd workflow
nodes.spec: Run a small end-to-end orchestration smoke where Linux and Mac orchestrators sync the same qd export, claim distinct ready nodes, post coordination status, and avoid local database conflicts.
nodes.acceptance:
Linux orchestrator qd ready and status outputs are recorded
Mac orchestrator qd ready and status outputs are recorded
Both orchestrators post structured status in the coordination issue
A claimed Mac node and claimed Linux node are visible in exported qd state
No .qd/qd.db file is committed

nodes.title: Define the compositor adapter contract
nodes.spec: Add a Rust compositor adapter contract for remote output lifecycle and workspace/session binding without depending on Hyprland directly.
nodes.acceptance:
Adapter trait and typed errors compile under workspace lints
Contract includes create, configure, park, remove, list, reconcile, and bind operations
No capture, encode, transport, or session code depends on Hyprland types
Unit tests or compile-time examples cover the expected lifecycle

nodes.title: Capture Hyprland output and workspace fixtures
nodes.spec: Capture real Hyprland JSON fixtures for monitors, workspaces, clients, and relevant event streams on the Linux host.
nodes.acceptance:
Fixtures are captured from the reference Linux host
Commands are recorded in evidence/m1-hyprland-fixtures/commands.log
Fixture files are committed under an appropriate test fixture path
Fixtures do not contain secrets or personal data
Parser follow-up needs are documented if fixture shape differs from expectations

nodes.title: Implement the Hyprland command and event layer
nodes.spec: Implement a mockable command/event layer around Hyprland IPC and hyprctl JSON using captured fixtures.
nodes.acceptance:
Command executor is injectable for tests
Fixture-backed tests cover monitor and workspace parsing
Errors are typed and preserve command context
No product code shells out directly except through the command layer

nodes.title: Implement Hyprland remote output lifecycle
nodes.spec: Create, configure, park, remove, and reconcile named Hyprland remote outputs through the adapter contract.
nodes.acceptance:
A named remote output can be created on the Linux host
Output can be parked without disrupting the developer session
Output can be removed idempotently
Restart or stale-output reconciliation behavior is typed and tested
Hardware evidence includes command logs and Hyprland state before and after lifecycle operations

nodes.title: Expose remote display control through hostd and madobectl
nodes.spec: Wire the compositor adapter into hostd and add madobectl commands for remote display status and lifecycle smoke operations.
nodes.acceptance:
madobectl exposes display status and lifecycle commands
hostd uses the adapter contract rather than Hyprland internals
CLI output is deterministic enough for tests
Linux hardware validation commands are documented

nodes.title: Validate M1 remote display ownership on Linux hardware
nodes.spec: Run the full M1 remote output lifecycle on the reference Linux host and preserve evidence for audit.
nodes.acceptance:
Remote output lifecycle succeeds on hardware
Restart or reconciliation scenario is exercised or blocked with exact condition
evidence/m1-linux-hardware-validation contains commands, host logs, Hyprland JSON, and notes
Findings are filed for any flaky or unproven behavior

nodes.title: Audit M1 remote display ownership
nodes.spec: Audit the M1 implementation, evidence, and DAG shape before considering remote display ownership complete.
nodes.acceptance:
qd audit is started for M1 implementation nodes
P0/P1 findings are resolved or explicitly dispositioned
Hardware evidence is reviewed
No direct Hyprland dependency leaks outside the adapter boundary
qd audit pass reports are recorded

nodes.title: Define capture frame metadata and evidence contract
nodes.spec: Define Rust types for captured frame metadata, DMA-BUF descriptors, format/modifier data, damage, sync, and evidence serialization.
nodes.acceptance:
Capture metadata types compile under workspace lints
Types represent format, modifier, size, damage, sync, and timestamp fields
Serialization fixtures or golden debug output exist
No platform API is claimed beyond documented evidence

nodes.title: Probe PipeWire and XDPH capture capabilities
nodes.spec: Probe PipeWire and XDPH on the reference Linux host for named remote output capture capability and persist capability metadata.
nodes.acceptance:
PipeWire and portal versions are recorded
Available capture nodes and formats are dumped
Named remote output capture path is identified or blocked with evidence
Artifacts are saved under evidence/m2-pipewire-probe

nodes.title: Capture one remote-output frame with metadata
nodes.spec: Capture one frame from the Hyprland remote output and persist metadata proving the buffer path, format, modifier, and sync properties.
nodes.acceptance:
One frame can be captured from the remote output
Metadata includes size, format, modifier, timestamp, and sync mode
CPU readback is not claimed unless directly evidenced
Artifacts are saved under evidence/m2-capture-one-frame
nodes.status_reason:
[2026-07-03T18:29:17.489Z] Blocked by external-dependency: Named remote output capture through PipeWire/XDPH is not proven by m2-pipewire-probe; no stable noninteractive XDPH named-output selector was found and no PipeWire video stream node appears before a user-consented ScreenCast session.
Needed: Prove a user-consented portal stream maps to the madobe-owned Hyprland output, or prove a direct named-output capture path with format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata.
[2026-07-03T20:18:05.519Z] Blocked by external-dependency: Portal ScreenCast selection for the madobe-owned Hyprland output is still not proven: CreateSession returned a request path, SelectSources failed with Access denied from the non-owning bus probe, and PipeWire dumps showed no video/portal/madobe stream node. Direct grim -o can target the named output when launched with the live Wayland socket, but it only yields PNG output and does not provide DMA-BUF format, modifier, timestamp, or sync metadata.
Needed: Implement or run a portal client that owns org.freedesktop.portal.Request.Response, receives user consent for the madobe output, opens the returned PipeWire remote, and records stream metadata; or implement an equivalent direct capture path exposing format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata.
[2026-07-03T20:48:56.340Z] Blocked by external-dependency: A real portal ScreenCast request client now receives CreateSession and extracts the session_handle, but SelectSources produced no response within the bounded 30-second wait. No Start response, stream ids, OpenPipeWireRemote fd, or PipeWire video/portal/madobe stream metadata were obtained.
Needed: Determine why SelectSources does not complete for the node-scoped Hyprland output. Provide a visible user-consent/chooser flow with parent-window context or equivalent portal client behavior, then capture Start streams and OpenPipeWireRemote metadata; or use a direct capture API exposing format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata.
[2026-07-03T21:26:20.111Z] Blocked by external-dependency: Visible portal chooser appears for the ScreenCast SelectSources request and targeted keyboard automation was attempted, but SelectSources still timed out after the bounded 70-second wait. No Start response, stream ids, OpenPipeWireRemote fd, or PipeWire video/portal/madobe stream metadata were obtained.
Needed: Provide reliable portal chooser approval, manual or automated, that returns SelectSources and Start streams for the madobe output; or use a direct capture API exposing format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata.
[2026-07-03T21:50:52.480Z] m2-direct-capture-api-spike identified and live-probed a viable non-portal direct Wayland capture implementation path using ext_output_image_capture_source_manager_v1 plus ext_image_copy_capture_manager_v1 against the generated wl_output. This unblocks m2-capture-one-frame for an implicit-sync direct client implementation; it does not claim a frame has already been captured.
[2026-07-03T22:37:23.768Z] Blocked by external-dependency: Direct ext-image-copy capture reached constraints and timestamp/damage but returned frame.failed reason 0 instead of ready after render-node and card-node DMA-BUF allocation attempts.
Needed: Allocate with a driver-native NVIDIA modifier path or adjust the compositor/protocol path to accept the implicit-modifier dumb-buffer PRIME fd, then rerun until ext_image_copy_capture_frame_v1.ready is emitted.
[2026-07-03T22:37:24.401Z] Blocked by external-dependency: Direct ext-image-copy capture reached constraints and timestamp/damage but returned frame.failed reason 0 instead of ready after render-node and card-node DMA-BUF allocation attempts.
Needed: Allocate with a driver-native NVIDIA modifier path or adjust the compositor/protocol path to accept the implicit-modifier dumb-buffer PRIME fd, then rerun until ext_image_copy_capture_frame_v1.ready is emitted.
[2026-07-03T23:01:50.099Z] m2-native-dmabuf-allocation-proof proved a driver-native GBM DMA-BUF allocation path on /dev/dri/renderD128 with the advertised NVIDIA block-linear modifier and ext_image_copy_capture_frame_v1.ready=true; the prior dumb-buffer PRIME failure was allocator-specific.

nodes.title: Encode captured frames with NVENC AV1
nodes.spec: Add the initial isolated NVENC boundary and encode captured sample frames to an AV1 artifact on the reference Linux host.
nodes.acceptance:
Unsafe FFI, if needed, is isolated to an encode-nv-sys boundary
Safe encode API exposes only product-level settings
A captured sample is encoded to an artifact
Driver, GPU, encoder settings, and command logs are recorded
Audit focus includes unsafe boundary and artifact validity

nodes.title: Validate the Linux GPU video path evidence bundle
nodes.spec: Bundle and validate M2 capture and encode artifacts so downstream Mac decode work can consume stable samples.
nodes.acceptance:
Evidence bundle includes capture metadata, encoded sample, hashes, logs, and notes
Sample artifact can be consumed by a non-Linux decoder later
All unproven performance or zero-copy claims are explicitly absent or filed as findings
qd findings exist for any known gaps

nodes.title: Audit M2 GPU video path
nodes.spec: Audit M2 capture, encode, unsafe boundaries, and evidence before treating Linux video path samples as reliable inputs to M3.
nodes.acceptance:
Capture evidence is reviewed
NVENC unsafe boundary is reviewed
P0/P1 findings are resolved or dispositioned
Downstream sample artifact expectations are documented
qd audit pass reports are recorded

nodes.title: Define encoded video frame protocol fixtures
nodes.spec: Define protocol types and golden fixtures for encoded frame metadata shared by the Linux sender and Mac receiver.
nodes.acceptance:
Rust protocol types compile under workspace lints
Golden fixtures cover frame id, timestamps, codec, dimensions, keyframe flag, and payload hash
Swift mirror requirements are documented
Round-trip tests or golden-vector tests exist

nodes.title: Implement QUIC video lane skeleton
nodes.spec: Implement the first QUIC session and video lane skeleton with loopback tests and telemetry hooks, without depending on real capture or decode.
nodes.acceptance:
Loopback sender and receiver tests pass
Video lane carries framed sample payloads
Telemetry records send and receive timestamps
Transport errors are typed
No live network claim is made without cross-device validation

nodes.title: Mirror encoded frame metadata in Swift
nodes.spec: Add Swift-side encoded frame metadata models and tests that consume the Rust-authored golden fixtures.
nodes.acceptance:
Swift tests read or mirror the golden fixture semantics
Tuist/Xcode tests pass on macOS
Fixture mismatch behavior is tested
Mac evidence includes xcodebuild log

nodes.title: Decode an AV1 sample with VideoToolbox
nodes.spec: Decode a Linux-produced or checked-in AV1 sample with VideoToolbox on the MacBook and record decode capability and timing evidence.
nodes.acceptance:
VideoToolbox AV1 capability is recorded
Sample hash is recorded
Decode succeeds or exact unsupported-condition blocker is filed
Decode timing report is saved under evidence/m3-videotoolbox-decode-sample
Xcode or command-line validation log is recorded

nodes.title: Present decoded frames with a Metal renderer skeleton
nodes.spec: Add a minimal Metal renderer path in the macOS app shell that can present decoded/sample frames and record render timing.
nodes.acceptance:
Renderer path builds and tests on macOS
A visible frame or test pattern is presented
Render timing report is produced
Screenshot evidence is saved when practical
No streaming latency claim is made until cross-device validation

nodes.title: Run the first cross-device video smoke
nodes.spec: Coordinate Linux host sender and Mac client receiver over the LAN using the cross-device validation runbook, with logs and result JSON from both sides.
nodes.acceptance:
Both orchestrators sync the same branch and commits
Linux host command and logs are recorded
Mac client command and logs are recorded
result.json records frames sent, decoded, and presented where available
Failures create qd findings or prerequisite nodes rather than hidden notes

nodes.title: Audit M3 Mac video endpoint
nodes.spec: Audit M3 protocol, transport, Mac decode/render evidence, and cross-device smoke results before considering the Mac video endpoint complete.
nodes.acceptance:
Protocol and Swift fixture compatibility are reviewed
Mac decode/render evidence is reviewed
Cross-device evidence is reviewed if available
P0/P1 findings are resolved or dispositioned
qd audit pass reports are recorded

nodes.title: Validate M1 workspace session binding on Linux hardware
nodes.spec: Validate that a disposable workspace or session can be bound or moved to a madobe-owned remote Hyprland output on the reference Linux host, then cleaned up without disturbing developer-session monitors.
nodes.acceptance:
A node-scoped madobe remote output is created on Linux hardware
A disposable workspace or session is bound or moved to that output with live Hyprland evidence
Redacted Hyprland monitor and workspace JSON is recorded before, during, and after validation
Cleanup removes only the madobe-owned output and restores developer-session state
Findings are filed for any flaky or unproven binding behavior

nodes.title: Prove remote-output capture stream selection
nodes.spec: Prove whether a user-consented XDPH/PipeWire stream can be mapped to a madobe-owned Hyprland remote output, or document the direct named-output capture blocker with enough evidence to unblock or refine one-frame capture.
nodes.acceptance:
A node-scoped madobe remote output is created on Linux hardware
A user-consented portal ScreenCast session is attempted and its PipeWire stream metadata is captured
Evidence proves whether the stream maps to the madobe-owned output, including redacted Hyprland, portal, and PipeWire state
If portal mapping cannot be proven, direct named-output capture options and exact blockers are documented
Artifacts are saved under evidence/m2-capture-stream-proof and reports/qd/m2-capture-stream-proof
m2-capture-one-frame is unblocked only if format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata can be obtained or directly derived from the proven stream path

nodes.title: Prove portal ScreenCast client lifecycle
nodes.spec: Implement or run a real portal ScreenCast request client that owns the request lifecycle, waits for org.freedesktop.portal.Request.Response, accepts user consent, calls OpenPipeWireRemote, and persists stream/node metadata for the madobe-owned Hyprland output.
nodes.acceptance:
Uses the node-scoped madobe output and disposable workspace setup with cleanup evidence
Captures the portal response and returned stream ids from a consented ScreenCast session, or documents the exact manual UI or environment blocker
Calls OpenPipeWireRemote or records the exact blocker before that call
Captures PipeWire stream or node metadata sufficient to decide whether m2-capture-one-frame can proceed
Does not add third-party Rust dependencies without a separate dependency-policy finding or node
Artifacts are saved under evidence/m2-portal-screencast-client-proof and reports/qd/m2-portal-screencast-client-proof

nodes.title: Prove portal chooser interaction path
nodes.spec: Determine whether the remaining XDPH SelectSources timeout is caused by missing visible chooser or parent-window interaction, or whether the portal selection path is blocked on the reference Linux host even with a proper visible client flow.
nodes.acceptance:
Uses the node-scoped madobe output and disposable workspace setup with cleanup evidence
Runs or implements a visible portal client flow with a parent or window token if supported by available APIs or tools
Captures whether the chooser appears, whether it can be selected and approved autonomously, and whether SelectSources returns
If SelectSources returns, continues through Start, returned stream ids, OpenPipeWireRemote, and PipeWire metadata capture
If the flow cannot complete autonomously, records the exact UI manual action or portal-side limitation and keeps m2-capture-one-frame blocked
Does not add third-party Rust dependencies without a separate dependency-policy finding or node
Artifacts are saved under evidence/m2-portal-chooser-interaction-proof and reports/qd/m2-portal-chooser-interaction-proof

nodes.title: Prove portal manual consent frame path
nodes.spec: Run the visible xdg-desktop-portal ScreenCast chooser flow with explicit manual approval or available click automation, then continue through SelectSources, Start, stream ids, OpenPipeWireRemote, and PipeWire stream metadata if consent succeeds.
nodes.acceptance:
Uses a node-scoped madobe output and disposable workspace with cleanup evidence
Documents the exact manual action or automation used to approve the chooser
If SelectSources returns, continues through Start, stream ids, OpenPipeWireRemote, and PipeWire metadata capture
If approval still cannot complete, records the exact UI, portal, or input-automation limitation
Does not claim capture metadata unless size, format, modifier, timestamp, and sync evidence exists
Artifacts are saved under evidence/m2-portal-manual-consent-frame-proof and reports/qd/m2-portal-manual-consent-frame-proof

nodes.title: Spike direct Hyprland capture API path
nodes.spec: Investigate and minimally probe a non-portal direct capture path for the generated Hyprland output that can expose frame size, format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata.
nodes.acceptance:
Identifies candidate direct capture APIs or tools available on the reference Linux host
Runs a bounded probe against a node-scoped madobe output where possible
Records whether size, format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata are available
If viable, documents the exact implementation path for m2-capture-one-frame
If not viable, records why available tools or APIs cannot provide the required metadata
Artifacts are saved under evidence/m2-direct-capture-api-spike and reports/qd/m2-direct-capture-api-spike

nodes.title: Probe VideoToolbox AV1 decode capability on macOS
nodes.spec: Probe the local macOS VideoToolbox/CoreMedia runtime for AV1 decode support and preserve evidence that the Mac endpoint can evaluate the codec path before sample decode validation is available.
nodes.acceptance:
A deterministic Swift or XCTest probe records kCMVideoCodecType_AV1 / av01 VideoToolbox hardware decode support, or the exact unsupported condition
Mac environment, Xcode, Swift, and command evidence are saved under evidence/m3-videotoolbox-capability-probe
The probe runs under the macOS check path or is directly documented with command evidence
m3-videotoolbox-decode-sample remains responsible for actual encoded sample decode after M2 provides or checks in a valid frame

nodes.title: Prove native DMA-BUF allocation for direct capture
nodes.spec: Prove a driver-native DMA-BUF allocation path that can satisfy Hyprland ext-image-copy capture constraints for the generated output after dumb-buffer PRIME submission returned frame.failed.
nodes.acceptance:
Uses a node-scoped generated Hyprland output with cleanup evidence
Records allocator dependencies and whether the Nix dev shell needs pkg-config, libgbm, EGL, or another native allocation dependency
Allocates a DMA-BUF against the advertised capture device or a justified same-driver device using an advertised format/modifier when possible
Submits the buffer through zwp_linux_dmabuf_v1 to ext_image_copy_capture_frame_v1
Evidence includes advertised device, allocation node/device, selected format, modifier, stride/planes, timestamp/damage, ready/fail result, and cleanup
If capture still fails, records the exact compositor, protocol, driver, or allocator condition and proposes the next split
Artifacts are saved under evidence/m2-native-dmabuf-allocation-proof and reports/qd/m2-native-dmabuf-allocation-proof

nodes.title: m2-video-path-validation does not prove direct DMA-BUF import into NVENC
nodes.spec: The stable sample was produced through a grim PNG/RGB capture and ffmpeg/NVENC process boundary with format=nv12; see evidence/m2-video-path-validation/bundle-manifest.json explicitNonClaims.
nodes.acceptance: Finding is addressed and verified.
nodes.status_reason: Promoted from finding eed2a910-d829-46e8-af64-837ed01ed78e on node m2-video-path-validation.

nodes.title: m2-video-path-validation does not prove downstream Mac decode/render
nodes.spec: sample-av1.ivf probes as AV1/IVF on Linux, but no VideoToolbox decode or Metal render has been executed yet; see evidence/m2-video-path-validation/bundle-manifest.json.
nodes.acceptance: Finding is addressed and verified.
nodes.status_reason: Promoted from finding 28358c32-733e-4e8e-8f6b-f5c2d4d13c99 on node m2-video-path-validation.

nodes.title: m2-video-path-validation does not validate HDR or color-management behavior
nodes.spec: ffprobe reports yuv420p with color_range=tv and unknown color_space/color_transfer/color_primaries; see evidence/m2-video-path-validation/bundle-manifest.json encodedSample.
nodes.acceptance: Finding is addressed and verified.
nodes.status_reason: Promoted from finding fcaaae62-2275-44ce-ae76-a99e45e06cb8 on node m2-video-path-validation.

nodes.title: m2-video-path-validation does not measure streaming latency or performance
nodes.spec: The bundle contains a one-frame artifact and command logs only. No end-to-end streaming latency, encode latency, throughput, or CPU/GPU utilization claims are proven.
nodes.acceptance: Finding is addressed and verified.
nodes.status_reason: Promoted from finding 77eec369-a6fe-4261-8dcf-1fbd8a575ca7 on node m2-video-path-validation.

nodes.title: m2-video-path-validation does not prove no CPU readback after grim capture
nodes.spec: The encode sample materializes captured-sample.png and captured-sample.rgb before NVENC encode, so no zero-copy or no-CPU-readback claim should be made for the grim-based sample.
nodes.acceptance: Finding is addressed and verified.
nodes.status_reason: Promoted from finding a2842577-9a8b-4601-862b-0cddb671c832 on node m2-video-path-validation.

nodes.title: Add Mac encoded video receive queue validation
nodes.spec: Add Swift client-side receive queue validation for encoded video frames using synthetic in-memory samples so the Mac endpoint can validate payload size, payload hash, ordering, and drop behavior before live cross-device smoke.
nodes.acceptance:
Swift client core validates incoming encoded frame metadata against payload byte count and SHA-256 payload hash
Queue depth is bounded to one ready frame and accepting a newer frame drops stale queued frame evidence deterministically
Receive and dequeue timeline events can be emitted as Codable evidence for later latency reports
XCTest coverage exercises valid enqueue/dequeue, byte-count mismatch, hash mismatch, stale-frame drop, and stable timeline JSON
Evidence is saved under evidence/m3-mac-video-receive-queue and reports under reports/qd/m3-mac-video-receive-queue
No live network, Linux capture, VideoToolbox decode, Metal render, or cross-device latency claim is made
nodes.context:
platform:macos
owner:macos-orchestrator
area:Swift client receive queue
validation:local-xcode

findings.title: Hosted macOS CI failed during bootstrap
findings.evidence: GitHub Actions run 28631597630: linux job passed, macos job failed in 'nix develop -c just check'. URL: https://github.com/cat-cave/madobe/actions/runs/28631597630/job/84909466577

findings.title: Direct capture frame never reached ready
findings.expected: The capture frame should emit ext_image_copy_capture_frame_v1.ready so the node can claim a completed frame and protocol-ready implicit synchronization.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a driver-native allocator that can create an advertised NVIDIA block-linear DMA-BUF modifier, or adjust the compositor/protocol path to accept the implicit-modifier dumb-buffer PRIME fd.

findings.title: m2-video-path-validation does not prove direct DMA-BUF import into NVENC
findings.evidence: The stable sample was produced through a grim PNG/RGB capture and ffmpeg/NVENC process boundary with format=nv12; see evidence/m2-video-path-validation/bundle-manifest.json explicitNonClaims.

findings.title: m2-video-path-validation does not prove downstream Mac decode/render
findings.evidence: sample-av1.ivf probes as AV1/IVF on Linux, but no VideoToolbox decode or Metal render has been executed yet; see evidence/m2-video-path-validation/bundle-manifest.json.

findings.title: m2-video-path-validation does not validate HDR or color-management behavior
findings.evidence: ffprobe reports yuv420p with color_range=tv and unknown color_space/color_transfer/color_primaries; see evidence/m2-video-path-validation/bundle-manifest.json encodedSample.

findings.title: m2-video-path-validation does not measure streaming latency or performance
findings.evidence: The bundle contains a one-frame artifact and command logs only. No end-to-end streaming latency, encode latency, throughput, or CPU/GPU utilization claims are proven.

findings.title: m2-video-path-validation does not prove no CPU readback after grim capture
findings.evidence: The encode sample materializes captured-sample.png and captured-sample.rgb before NVENC encode, so no zero-copy or no-CPU-readback claim should be made for the grim-based sample.

node_notes.text:
Blocked by external-dependency: Named remote output capture through PipeWire/XDPH is not proven by m2-pipewire-probe; no stable noninteractive XDPH named-output selector was found and no PipeWire video stream node appears before a user-consented ScreenCast session.
Needed: Prove a user-consented portal stream maps to the madobe-owned Hyprland output, or prove a direct named-output capture path with format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata.

Blocked by external-dependency: Portal ScreenCast selection for the madobe-owned Hyprland output is still not proven: CreateSession returned a request path, SelectSources failed with Access denied from the non-owning bus probe, and PipeWire dumps showed no video/portal/madobe stream node. Direct grim -o can target the named output when launched with the live Wayland socket, but it only yields PNG output and does not provide DMA-BUF format, modifier, timestamp, or sync metadata.
Needed: Implement or run a portal client that owns org.freedesktop.portal.Request.Response, receives user consent for the madobe output, opens the returned PipeWire remote, and records stream metadata; or implement an equivalent direct capture path exposing format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata.

Blocked by external-dependency: A real portal ScreenCast request client now receives CreateSession and extracts the session_handle, but SelectSources produced no response within the bounded 30-second wait. No Start response, stream ids, OpenPipeWireRemote fd, or PipeWire video/portal/madobe stream metadata were obtained.
Needed: Determine why SelectSources does not complete for the node-scoped Hyprland output. Provide a visible user-consent/chooser flow with parent-window context or equivalent portal client behavior, then capture Start streams and OpenPipeWireRemote metadata; or use a direct capture API exposing format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata.

Blocked by external-dependency: Visible portal chooser appears for the ScreenCast SelectSources request and targeted keyboard automation was attempted, but SelectSources still timed out after the bounded 70-second wait. No Start response, stream ids, OpenPipeWireRemote fd, or PipeWire video/portal/madobe stream metadata were obtained.
Needed: Provide reliable portal chooser approval, manual or automated, that returns SelectSources and Start streams for the madobe output; or use a direct capture API exposing format, modifier, timestamp, and sync metadata.

m2-direct-capture-api-spike identified and live-probed a viable non-portal direct Wayland capture implementation path using ext_output_image_capture_source_manager_v1 plus ext_image_copy_capture_manager_v1 against the generated wl_output. This unblocks m2-capture-one-frame for an implicit-sync direct client implementation; it does not claim a frame has already been captured.

Blocked by external-dependency: Direct ext-image-copy capture reached constraints and timestamp/damage but returned frame.failed reason 0 instead of ready after render-node and card-node DMA-BUF allocation attempts.
Needed: Allocate with a driver-native NVIDIA modifier path or adjust the compositor/protocol path to accept the implicit-modifier dumb-buffer PRIME fd, then rerun until ext_image_copy_capture_frame_v1.ready is emitted.

Blocked by external-dependency: Direct ext-image-copy capture reached constraints and timestamp/damage but returned frame.failed reason 0 instead of ready after render-node and card-node DMA-BUF allocation attempts.
Needed: Allocate with a driver-native NVIDIA modifier path or adjust the compositor/protocol path to accept the implicit-modifier dumb-buffer PRIME fd, then rerun until ext_image_copy_capture_frame_v1.ready is emitted.

m2-native-dmabuf-allocation-proof proved a driver-native GBM DMA-BUF allocation path on /dev/dri/renderD128 with the advertised NVIDIA block-linear modifier and ext_image_copy_capture_frame_v1.ready=true; the prior dumb-buffer PRIME failure was allocator-specific.
